Title: Limes inferior
Author: Janusz A. Zajdel
My Review:
There is barely anything out there that can make your jaw drop when it comes to foreseeing the future as far ahead and as accurately as Limes Inferior does. While many, especially dystopian titles were meant to do so, this one does in such visceral fashion it's just scary - with descriptions of fictional reality as if an analysis of the contemporary and near-future world written nearly just 4 decades ago.

Even if parts may feel bit off-putting, with the eerie conclusion and relatively slow-burn start, it ultimately became my favorite dystopian novel that can actually be mistaken for reality. The story's progression is impressive with far-reaching descriptions and the ultimate wrap of what it's about.

Title: Lolita
Author: Vladimir Nabokov
My Review: Wonderfully written, especially and predominantly as for the first half of the book; as a whole it feels complete but I just don't see too much value in it. Most of it is well constructed but apart from revolving around a taboo as a centerpoint it just does not deliver anything above what a typical novel should and does.
